duration.FP1: Four-year undergraduate LLB: 4 years (5-year stream: 5 years for continuing students only).

curriculum_graduate.FP4.1: Graduate LLB stream (LP001): Preliminary, Intermediate, and Final levels as prescribed.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.2: Research component and Community Service are compulsory. Research component weighted 36 NQF credits.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.3: Final Level electives are 9 NQF credits each.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.4: Minimum 36 NQF credits from Final Level electives. Two kinds: lectures-and-examination electives, and seminars-and-research-paper electives.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.5: Every Final Level student must do at least one seminars and research paper elective, or an Independent Research Paper of 8000 words instead.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.6: Maximum credits for elective courses in Final Level: 54 NQF credits.
curriculum_graduate.FP4.7: Intermediate Level students may register for DOL4500F/S Legal Practice if >=65% average in Preliminary Level law courses and admitted by UCT Law Clinic Director.

curriculum_undergraduate.FP5.1: Four-year undergraduate LLB (LB002) curriculum as prescribed.
curriculum_undergraduate.FP5.2: Candidate must pass a numeracy test prescribed by Senate, OR complete MAM1013F Law That Counts. Candidate may not attempt numeracy test a second time.
curriculum_undergraduate.FP5.3: Curriculum for undergraduate LLB stream includes non-law courses in first and second year from other faculties.
curriculum_undergraduate.FP5.3.1: A candidate who has passed the numeracy test with a score of at least 66% must complete an additional semester course offered in another faculty.

skills_component.FP6.1: All streams have a skills component at each level. Compulsory.
skills_component.FP6.2: Skills include: writing, computer skills, problem solving, analysis, research, oral presentation.
skills_component.FP6.3: First year emphasis: writing skills, problem solving, reading skills.
skills_component.FP6.4: Second year emphasis: problem solving, analysis including critical analysis, oral presentation.
skills_component.FP6.5: Third year: essay, opinion, attend tutorials.
skills_component.FP6.6: Fourth year: Integrative Assessment Moot (DOL4000H) — capstone.

progress_graduate.FP7.1: Only one half course outstanding from Preliminary → may proceed to Intermediate. Only one course (or two half courses) outstanding from Intermediate → may proceed to Final. Senate permission required.
progress_graduate.FP7.2: Failed more than one half course at Preliminary, or more than one course (two half courses) at Intermediate/Final: may not register for full set but may register limited load.

progress_undergraduate.FP8.1: Only one half course outstanding from first/second year → may proceed. Only one course (two half courses) from third year → may proceed to fourth. Senate permission. Applies to both law and non-law courses.
progress_undergraduate.FP8.2: Failed more than one half course in first/second year, or more than one course in third year: limited registration.

general_progress.FP10.1: Maximum courses per year: (i) 9 half courses at Preliminary, (ii) 12 half courses at Intermediate, (iii) 14 half courses at Final.
general_progress.FP10.2: Notwithstanding FP10.1, students may take Humanities courses in Summer Term.

readmission.FP11.1: May be refused re-registration if fails: (i) equivalent of 4 half courses at any level, OR (ii) all courses within prescribed time plus one year.
readmission.FP11.2: Five-year undergraduate: refused if fails equivalent of 3 half courses.
readmission.FP12: Refused students may appeal to Faculty Readmission Appeal Committee. Decision is final.

assessment.FP13.1: Failure to write exam = failed course for readmission purposes.
assessment.FP13.2: Senate may grant permission to write on different day for medical, religious, or political reasons. Application to Dean within 7 days.
assessment.FP14: Sub-minimum of 45% in every University examination (including research paper component of Final Level electives).
assessment.FP15.1: Final year students may request oral exam if scored 47%+ overall or 45%+ in final exam. Not for seminars/research paper electives.
assessment.FP15.2: Minimum 72 hours notice for oral examination.
assessment.FP16.1: Supplementary exams at Senate discretion if sufficiently high standard in 45-49% range, or if candidate is in final year and able to qualify for graduation.

deferred_exams.FP17.1: Deferred exams on medical or compassionate grounds.
deferred_exams.FP17.3: Student who becomes ill during exam must proceed to Student Wellness Service.
deferred_exams.FP17.4: Misreading timetable is not grounds for deferral.

distinction.FP18.1: Cum laude: (i) complete in minimum time without failing, (ii) average 70% in all law courses at UCT, (iii) First Class pass in at least 6 full law courses.
distinction.FP18.2: Magna cum laude: (i) complete in minimum time without failing, (ii) average 75% in all law courses at UCT, (iii) First Class pass in at least 9 full law courses.
